headline: Apptronik first agent baseline. Established/exemplary boundary-case. February 10 2026: closed $520M Series A-X extension bringing total Series A to $935M at $5.3B valuation, co-led by B Capital and Google with Mercedes-Benz, PEAK6, AT&T Ventures, John Deere, and Qatar Investment Authority. Apollo robot in active commercial pilots at Mercedes-Benz factories, GXO Logistics warehouses, Jabil; Google testing alongside Mercedes-Benz. New humanoid model debut planned 2026; Austin facility expansion + new California office. POSITIVE: high-profile institutional governance scaffolding (Google, Mercedes-Benz, John Deere, B Capital boards). MIXED: Qatar Investment Authority backing introduces sovereign-wealth-fund accountability dimension; aggressive 2026 deployment timeline. Published 81.4 / Exemplary band — band-boundary placement at established/exemplary threshold (81). The reported scan composite of 76.4 was incorrect; canonical index value is 81.4. Sub-threshold drift (SYS +0.5 considered for institutional-governance scaffolding offset by EQU -0.5 considered for QIA sovereign-wealth backing). Recommend confirm at 81.4 with first-baseline rationale.
key_evidence: February 10 2026: Apptronik closed $520M Series A-X extension; total Series A = $935M at $5.3B valuation. Co-led by B Capital and Google with Mercedes-Benz, PEAK6, AT&T Ventures, John Deere, Qatar Investment Authority.
Apollo robot active commercial pilots: Mercedes-Benz factories, GXO Logistics warehouses, Jabil. Google co-testing with Mercedes-Benz.
New humanoid model debut planned 2026; Austin facility expansion + new California office.
High-profile institutional governance scaffolding: Google, Mercedes-Benz (auto-safety mature), John Deere (industrial-safety mature), B Capital (institutional VC).
Qatar Investment Authority backing introduces sovereign-wealth-fund accountability dimension — potential conflict-of-interest with QIA strategic interests.

Apptronik signatory status on 2022 anti-weaponization pledge: NOT confirmed in available sources — pledge signatories were Boston Dynamics, Agility, ANYbotics, Clearpath, Open Robotics, Unitree (Apptronik not on list).
No published Apollo model card or system card; humanoid commercial-deployment safety framework reliant on partner-side (Mercedes/GXO/Jabil) industrial-safety baselines.

evidence.finding: $935M Series A total; $5.3B valuation; investor base profile.

evidence.source: CNBC — Apptronik raises $520M at $5B valuation for Apollo
evidence.finding: Series A-X extension details; Mercedes-Benz/GXO/Jabil pilot operational confirmation.

evidence.source: Robotics and Automation News — Apptronik nears $1B in funding
evidence.finding: Capital deployment plan: facility expansion, new humanoid model 2026 debut, training-and-data-collection facilities.

scoring_rationale.AWR: 4.0 confirmed. A1 detection: multi-pilot deployment provides operational-issue surfacing; A4 amplification: institutional-governance scaffolding (Google, Mercedes, John Deere, B Capital boards) amplifies safety signals.
scoring_rationale.EMP: 4.0 confirmed. E1 affective resonance: Apollo design framing acknowledges human-coexistence; partner-side industrial-safety baselines (Mercedes, John Deere) extend empathic-regard architecture.
scoring_rationale.ACT: 4.0 confirmed. AC2 proportionality: structured pilot deployment scope; AC4 response: institutional-governance partners provide incident-response frameworks.
scoring_rationale.EQU: 3.5 confirmed. EQ2 distributional awareness: humanoid-deployment scale concentrates capability among well-resourced industrial partners; QIA sovereign-wealth backing introduces equity-question on global-distribution dimension.
scoring_rationale.BND: 4.0 confirmed. B3 scope clarity: industrial-deployment context with mature partners; B5 consent: factory/warehouse deployment context aligns with declared use cases. Anti-weaponization pledge non-signatory status is sub-threshold INT-erosive but does not drop BND.
scoring_rationale.ACC: 4.0 confirmed. AB2 transparency: funding-disclosure and pilot-deployment-disclosure functional; AB4 reparation: institutional-governance partners provide commercial-level reparation frameworks.
scoring_rationale.SYS: 4.0 confirmed. S1 causal reasoning: institutional-governance scaffolding (Google, Mercedes, John Deere, B Capital) operationalizes systemic-harm reasoning; multi-partner deployment extends systemic feedback.
scoring_rationale.INT: 4.0 confirmed. I1 consistency: declared safety/industrial-deployment commitments consistent with operational pilots; I2 non-performance: limited gap between marketing posture and operational deployment.

math_check.validator_check: Reconstruction yields 73.4 vs published 81.4. Discrepancy +8.0 — MATH-HYGIENE FLAG (significant; among the largest reconstruction discrepancies observed). Dimension means are anchored to evidence and consistent with band-placement evidence; published composite reconstructs to established band rather than exemplary. Recommend math-hygiene flag for data-team review per cluster protocol; do NOT propose composite change in evidence cycle. Boundary-case approved: published-exemplary placement sustained pending math-hygiene resolution; if reconstructed composite is canonical, entity is established-band (band-boundary case).

math_hygiene_flag.issue: Published composite 81.4 vs reconstructed 73.4 from dimension means. Discrepancy +8.0 points — significant, among the largest in current cluster. Published placement is exemplary (>=81); reconstructed placement is established (61-80).
math_hygiene_flag.action: Flag for data-team review per math-hygiene cluster protocol. Do NOT propose composite change in evidence cycle. Dimension means consistent with evidence base; published composite may reflect older scoring formula or dimension-weighting scheme.
math_hygiene_flag.evidence_basis_for_dimensions: All eight dimension means at 4.0 except EQU at 3.5; supported by Mercedes/GXO/Jabil pilot operational evidence + institutional-governance scaffolding. If exemplary-band is canonical, dimensions would need to be ~4.25 average, which is not supported by available evidence.

negativeSignals: Anti-weaponization pledge non-signatory status; Qatar Investment Authority sovereign-wealth backing introduces conflict-of-interest dimension; aggressive 2026 deployment + new-model-debut timeline; no published Apollo model card or system card; safety framework reliant on partner-side industrial-safety baselines rather than entity-level published framework.
positiveSignals: $935M Series A at $5.3B valuation; Google, Mercedes-Benz, John Deere, B Capital institutional-governance scaffolding; Apollo active commercial pilots at Mercedes-Benz, GXO, Jabil; new humanoid model 2026 debut; Austin facility expansion; mature partner industrial-safety baselines extend empathic-regard and accountability architecture.
sector_context: Robotics Labs / commercial-humanoid cluster. Cross-reference: Boston Dynamics (anti-weaponization pledge, Hyundai deployment), Agility (anti-weaponization pledge, Toyota Canada pilot), Figure AI (no pledge, BMW pilot), 1X Technologies (consumer pre-orders, OpenAI-backed), Tesla Optimus (no pledge, factory deployment + disclosure-gap). Apptronik distinguished by highest valuation + most-diverse institutional-governance scaffolding + multi-partner commercial pilots. Math-hygiene flag for data-team review on +8.0 reconstruction discrepancy.
open_actions_resolved: First baseline established with exemplary-band confirmation (boundary case)
$935M Series A and $5.3B valuation context absorbed
Math-hygiene flag for data-team review (+8.0 discrepancy)
open_actions_carried: Math-hygiene escalation: published 81.4 vs reconstructed 73.4 — significant cluster-pattern flag
Watch: Apollo model card, system card, or entity-level safety policy publication
Watch: any anti-weaponization pledge signing event
Watch: new-humanoid-model 2026 debut and safety framework
Watch: any humanoid-deployment harm or incident report
